Repo setup

Human-readable startup checklist

A copy-friendly startup guide for setting up new repositories with an AI-friendly, user-in-control structure. It helps users who are less familiar with the command line find the right setup commands, while still assuming they can install and configure developer tools such as Python, Docker, and Git hosting CLIs. Fill in the project context once, then copy commands shaped for your platform, shell, and Git hosting service. The checklist also includes downloadable starter files, tool checks, and “Copy to AI for help” prompts so you can set up the repo and start working with an agent such as Codex or Docker Sandbox.

Git first Starter files second Baseline commit third Agent work fourth

Before you start: this guide assumes you can open a terminal, sign in to your Git hosting service, and create or connect repositories for the account or organization you plan to use.

TeamNL Sport Science Centre
